title: Street Fighter 6

notes:
I heard people with way newer cards like 1080/3080 had this same issue with RE 4 Demo, updating their drivers to the latest Nvidia release fixed the issue for them.
But people with Kepler, 600-700-800-900 are stuck with 470 drivers. Newer cards can update to 500+ drivers.
Pretty sure DXGI is something not that important but still sucks we can even try the game at the lowest settings. 
GTX 680 here with 470 Drivers.
Made this report so people with old NVIDIA cards should think twice before trying the demo or buying the game. Good luck.
Tried Proton Experimental, 7.0, 8.0, and Glorious Eggroll.
title: Street Fighter 6

notes: Tried every version of proton and launch command I could find, tried various kernel versions, driver versions, and all of them result in getting the error "DX12-compatible DXGI adapter not found." The game works fine in windows on the same system, so this *must* be either a proton thing or a devuan thing. I'm stuck with Windows for now. Multiple dev teams owe me a day of my life.
